reasoning: The patient presents with recurrent SVT, lightheadedness, and falls, with recent onset of hip pain and headache.

symptoms.evidence: Patient called EMS for the symptoms earlier of lightheadedness and palpitations at home.
related_findings: ST elevations in II, III, V1-V3

children.evidence: Patient called EMS for the symptoms earlier of lightheadedness and palpitations at home.
Patient denied chest pain, no chest pressure, no palpitations, no shortness of breath.

symptoms.evidence: When nurse tried to reposition her, patient had pain in her right hip.
related_findings: nondisplaced comminuted fracture in the right superior pubic ramus

symptoms.evidence: Patient endorsed that when she stood up earlier she had felt pain in her entire right leg - her foot, ankle, leg, hip.
